import io
import string
import list

fn split_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut start = 0
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    let b = match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(x)
        x
      when None
        0
      end
    if b == 32 or b == 9
      if start < i
        let tok = string.substring(s, start, i)
        match string.parse_int(tok)
        when Some(v)
          result = list.push(result, v)
        when None
          ()
        end
      end
      start = i + 1
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if start < n
    let tok = string.substring(s, start, n)
    match string.parse_int(tok)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

fn find_pair(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<String>
  let n = xs.len()
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    mut j = i + 1
    while j < n
      let a = match xs.get(i) when Some(v) v when None 0 end
      let b = match xs.get(j) when Some(v) v when None 0 end
      if a + b == t
        return Some("found=#{i},#{j}")
      end
      j = j + 1
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  None
end

let line1 = match io.read_line() when Some(s) s when None "" end
let line2 = match io.read_line() when Some(s) s when None "" end

let nums = split_ints(string.trim(line1))
let target = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end

match find_pair(nums, target)
when Some(msg)
  println(msg)
when None
  println("none")
end
